content.problemStatement:
Chef is stuck in the wavey world of polynomials. You are given all NNN roots of a polynomial P(x)=‚ąŹi=1N(x‚ąíai)P(x) = \prod_{i=1}^N (x - a_i)P(x)=‚ąŹi=1N‚Äč(x‚ąíai‚Äč). The roots are pairwise distinct integers, but they are not given in any particular order.

To help Chef escape, you should answer QQQ queries (numbered 111 through QQQ). For each valid iii, in the iii-th query, you are given an integer xix_ixi‚Äč and you have to determine whether P(xi)P(x_i)P(xi‚Äč) is positive, negative or 000.
constraints: a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a single integer xix_ixi‚Äč describing the iii-th query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing the string `"POSITIVE"`, `"NEGATIVE"` or `"0"` (without quotes) describing the value of the polynomial for the iii-th query.
